description:
Goal
Make the selective-receive fixture stop timing out in compiler2 semantic drive after a clean rebuild, and then either run correctly or fail with a precise semantic/runtime blocker. The current fresh-build failure is no longer just an opaque >120s test hang: the backend-artifact interp test still fails to terminate, and the fresh `fz2 run` path now proves the compiler drive itself can burn its full 5s budget on this fixture.

Signal
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/timeout 30s target/debug/deps/fz-01415279b97da685 --exact compiler2::drive_test::compiler2_interp_runs_selective_receive_with_make_ref_from_backend_artifacts --nocapture` exits before timeout.
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/timeout 10s target/debug/fz2 --log-telemetry <log> run fixtures/receive_selective_refs/input.fz` exits before the compiler2 5s drive limit.
- Telemetry identifies and then eliminates the repeated activation/fact edge currently driving `20600` jobs with `1` pending at timeout.
- The fix does not reintroduce missing-return-as-none/any, retained-output shims, longer timeouts, or receive-specific semantic shortcuts.

Strategy
Start from the fresh `fz2 run` telemetry, which now shows a single-pending-job convergence failure instead of a purely runtime mystery. Identify the repeating activation/return-readiness frontier for the selective-receive fixture, repair the producer boundary, and then re-check whether any residual runtime receive bug remains once semantic convergence is honest.

Outro
No unbounded selective-receive drive, no hidden timeout dependency, no special receive wait shim, no broad any/none sentinel standing in for missing receive evidence, and no ticket text that pretends this is only a native/runtime bug.

comments.text: 2026-06-12 wider-suite refresh: even with compiler2_interp_runs_selective_receive_with_make_ref_from_backend_artifacts and enum_tier0_fixture_exercises_basic_operations skipped, /opt/homebrew/bin/timeout 300s cargo test --lib compiler2:: -- --test-threads=1 ... timed out while quiet at compiler2::port_interp_test::selective_receive_pinned_ref_out_of_order_server_replies. Treat the receive ticket as covering both the drive/native selective receive hang and the port-interp selective receive hang until telemetry proves separate root causes.

title: Compiler2 selective receive: stop semantic/native fixture timeout after return-readiness fixes
